questions.text: The average of a set of data is also called the:
questions.explanation: Arithmetic Mean is calculated by dividing the sum of observations by the total number of observations.

questions.text: The difference between the highest and lowest observations is called:
questions.explanation: Range gives an idea of the spread of the data.

questions.text: The observation that occurs most frequently in a data set is the:
questions.explanation: Mode represents the value with the highest frequency.

questions.text: The middle value of a data set when arranged in order is the:
questions.explanation: Median is the middle observation after sorting data in ascending or descending order.

questions.text: What is the probability of an event that is certain to happen?
questions.explanation: The probability of a sure event is always 1.

questions.text: When a die is thrown, how many possible outcomes are there?
questions.explanation: A standard die has six faces numbered 1 to 6.

questions.text: What is the mean of the first five natural numbers?
The probability of an impossible event is:
questions.explanation: An event that cannot happen has a probability of 0.

questions.text: A coin is tossed. What is the probability of getting a Head?
questions.explanation: There is 1 favorable outcome (Head) out of 2 total outcomes (Head, Tail).

questions.text: Find the range of the data: 2, 4, 8, 9, 11.
